title: [lichen_link_rx.c:257] Replay entry created before MIC verification
description:
Flow: lichen_link_rx() -> lichen_replay_get() before verify_mic()

In lichen_link_rx.c, the replay window allocation happens at line 257 (lichen_replay_get), but MIC verification doesn't happen until line 265. If MIC verification fails, the replay table entry still exists with an updated LRU timestamp.

Attack scenario:
1. Attacker sends malformed frame with valid signature but bad MIC
2. Frame passes signature verification (if signature_present=true)
3. lichen_replay_get() is called, updating/creating replay entry
4. MIC verification fails, function returns -LICHEN_EAUTH
5. Replay entry persists with fresh LRU timestamp

Impact: An attacker can keep replay entries 'warm' (prevent LRU eviction) for peers they want to target for replay attacks later. This is a minor information leak / DoS vector since it affects table eviction ordering.

Mitigation: Move lichen_replay_get() call after verify_mic() returns successfully. The replay window commit (lichen_replay_check) already happens at Step 7 after all validation, but the table entry allocation should also be deferred.
close_reason: Duplicate of project-LICHEN-0li1.48 (same issue: replay window before verification, R2 version has more detail)
